mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.50% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of 2016-08-15 indicates ultra-low accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.50%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth exceeding 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth acceleration does not correlate with positive 60-day SPX returns (Spearman ρ < 0.15 over 3-year rolling windows), or mechanism fails to predict direction in > 45% of test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while unemployment remains elevated above 4.5%, signaling weakening consumer demand that compresses earnings multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of RSAFS and UNRATE shows no statistical interaction (p > 0.10 on interaction term in logistic regression).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month while remaining below 96.0 (2017=100) indicates manufacturing weakness and supply-side constraints that reduce corporate capacity utilization and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO < 96.0 does not predict negative 5-day returns (Rank IC > -0.05), or mechanism shows directional accuracy below 48% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline below 1,100 thousand units annually while real GDP growth remains below 2.5% annualized, signaling demand weakness across both residential and aggregate sectors that reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal of low HOUST and low GDPC1 growth does not predict negative 60-day returns (Rank IC > -0.04), or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPI remains below 1.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate stays below 0.25%, indicating deflation risk and monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations through lower real discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction between low inflation and low rates shows no statistical significance (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 5.0% while payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 persons month-over-month, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and equity earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or joint labor market weakness signal shows Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth accelerates above 3% year-over-year while housing starts exceed 1,200 thousand units, signaling robust consumer and residential demand that drives corporate earnings expansion and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 48% of triggered instances, or the interaction between retail sales and housing demand shows no statistical significance (p > 0.14) in logistic regression.
